metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact template variable the new `code_review_novelty.md` prompt uses for the diff context AND the exact resolver call-site signature (e.g. a code-review-specific `_resolve_code_review_system(prompt_id, diff_text, cfg)` analogous to `_resolve_system` but binding `{'diff': diff_text}`), so the implementer does not have to choose between reusing the misleading `{{plan}}` var or inventing an undocumented convention.
findings.finding: The plan instructs that the code_review_novelty.md system prompt is resolved 'as PASS_NOVELTY resolves with plan_text via _resolve_system', but _resolve_system (plan_review/passes.py:261) hard-codes the template-vars dict as {"plan": plan}. A code_review_novelty.md prompt that uses {{diff}} (not {{plan}}) as its domain-context variable would render with the literal unsubstituted string '{{diff}}' — producing a broken prompt. The plan simultaneously says 'DO NOT invent a template-var convention' and 'the diff as domain context', without specifying how the template var is bound. If the implementer reuses {{plan}} injecting the diff into it, the variable name is semantically misleading and inconsistent with the prompt content; if a new resolver is written, the 'do not invent' constraint is violated.
findings.location: Design section: 'the novelty SYSTEM prompt is resolved with the diff as domain context (as PASS_NOVELTY resolves with plan_text via _resolve_system)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section: 'code_review_novelty.md uses {{diff}} as its domain-context template var; the runner wiring binds it via a code-review-specific resolver `_resolve_code_novelty_system(prompt_id, diff_text, cfg)` (a thin clone of `_resolve_system` passing `{"diff": diff_text}` to `prompts.resolve_prompt`) — one new function, not a shared abstraction.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the exact template variable name for the diff in `code_review_novelty.md` (e.g. `{{diff}}`) and specify whether the runner wiring calls `_resolve_system` (passing diff as the 'plan' positional arg) or `resolve_prompt` directly with `{'diff': diff_text}`.
findings.finding: The `code_review_novelty.md` prompt's template variable name for the diff is never declared. The plan says the system prompt is 'resolved with the diff as domain context (as PASS_NOVELTY resolves with plan_text via _resolve_system)' — but `_resolve_system` calls `prompts.resolve_prompt(prompt, {'plan': plan})`, injecting the key `'plan'`. If the code-review path reuses `_resolve_system` unchanged, the diff would be passed under the `'plan'` key (semantically wrong). If it calls `resolve_prompt` directly with `{'diff': diff}`, the template var is `{{diff}}` — but neither the call site nor the template var name is specified, leaving the runner-wiring protocol ambiguous for the implementor.
findings.location: Design section: 'the novelty SYSTEM prompt is resolved with the diff as domain context (as PASS_NOVELTY resolves with plan_text via _resolve_system)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile e51b's prompt front-matter spec ({{diff}}+{{prior_findings}} template vars) with the parent's explicit 'do NOT invent a template-var convention' directive; align e51b's AC to specify system-prompt context injection via _resolve_system + instructions-block append, matching the parent design.
findings.finding: child e51b specifies the code_review_novelty.md prompt front-matter with template vars {{diff}}+{{prior_findings}}, directly contradicting the parent's explicit instruction: "do NOT invent a template-var convention — the novelty SYSTEM prompt is resolved with the diff as domain context (as PASS_NOVELTY resolves with plan_text via _resolve_system), the surfaced prior findings are appended to the INSTRUCTIONS as a '## Prior-review findings (context)' block."
findings.location: child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06, Acceptance Criteria: 'code_review_novelty.md (front-matter: {{diff}}+{{prior_findings}}, outputs novelty, single_turn)'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove {{diff}} and {{prior_findings}} from e51b's front-matter spec. Replace with: system prompt resolved with diff as domain context via _resolve_system; prior findings appended to instructions as a '## Prior-review findings (context)' block — matching the pattern described in the parent and mirroring PASS_NOVELTY.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Qualify Success Criterion 1 to reflect that local convergence applies only when a session lifecycle exists (session id resolvable), and the uuid-fallback case is intentionally inert — consistent with the Design's explicit rationale.
findings.finding: Success Criterion 1 ('On a re-run... every prior low-priority SURFACED finding is stamped carried_from and NOT re-coached') is stated unconditionally, but Design's uuid-fallback rationale explicitly says local convergence is 'intentionally INERT' for bare `rebar review-code` invocations without a session lifecycle. The Success Criterion makes a promise the design consciously does not deliver in a common invocation mode.
findings.location: Success Criteria (criterion 1) vs Design section 'uuid-fallback rationale'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to Success Criterion 1: '(applies where a session lifecycle exists; bare invocations with no resolvable session id are intentionally inert per the uuid-fallback rationale)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RED-fixture or sample-scoring spike for `code_review_novelty.md` that fires the prompt against a small set of representative (repeated, novel, region-changed) code findings and asserts that novelty scores fall in the expected range before the gate is wired into production flow.
findings.finding: The new code_review_novelty.md prompt is asserted to produce well-calibrated novelty scores for code-review findings (correctly distinguishing novel vs. carried findings by file/line region semantics), but no spike, RED fixture, or pilot measurement is planned to validate prompt calibration before full build-out. Miscalibrated scores (e.g., over-novel → gate silently inert; under-novel → real findings dropped) would undermine the convergence guarantee with no observable failure signal.
findings.location: Design section — 'Runner wiring mirrors plan-review's novelty sub-call' / 'code_review_novelty.md' / Success Criteria SC1
findings.suggested_fix: Create a test fixture (e.g., tests/code_review/test_novelty_prompt_calibration.py) with 3–5 golden examples: (a) an identical repeated finding → expect novelty < threshold; (b) a finding on a changed region → novelty score irrelevant but recorded; (c) a genuinely new finding class → expect novelty > threshold. Run against the real model in CI (or a stub) and assert bucket placement matches expectation. This is the empirical probe that de-risks the prompt before full wiring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact pipeline insertion point where carryover findings (those with `carried_from` set) are excluded from the `code_review_coach_inputs` coaching set while remaining in the `surfaced` output — e.g. 'the `code_review_coach_inputs` step filters `surfaced` to `[f for f in surfaced if not f.get("carried_from")]` before computing applicable moves.'
findings.finding: The plan specifies that carryover findings 'are OMITTED from coaching (not re-coached); they remain surfaced.' However, the plan does not specify WHERE in the code-review gate pipeline this omission occurs. The existing `code_review_coach_inputs` step (workflow_ops.py) and `code_review_coach` step receive the `surfaced` list from `code_review_decide`. If carryover findings remain in `surfaced` (which the plan says they do), the coach step will pick up their criteria and generate coaching notes for them — violating the 'not re-coached' requirement. The plan names no step, no filter, and no output-schema change that strips carryover findings from the coaching input while leaving them in the surfaced output.
findings.location: Design section: 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from … are OMITTED from coaching (not re-coached); they remain surfaced.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify (or add) an eval-gated activation flag for the code-review novelty floor — explain why the adapted `cites_prior_location` predicate needs no discriminability proof before drops occur, or add a `code_review_novelty_drop_active`-style guard mirroring ADR 0008 Invariant 4 / plan-review's `novelty_drop_active`.
findings.finding: The plan omits the eval-gated activation flag that the directly-reused prior art (ADR 0008 / plan-review's rising floor) relies on to ensure the novelty signal actually discriminates before any drops occur, with no justification for why code-review novelty needs no such proof.
findings.location: Design section: 'No eval-gated activation flag.' / ADR 0008 Invariant 4 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py lines 192-204 (novelty_drop_active guard)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code_review_novelty_drop_active` config flag (default `false`) that keeps `region_gated_floor` inert until an eval has validated that the code-review novelty sub-call discriminates. Wire a `discriminates_novelty`-style eval spec for code-review novelty. This directly mirrors the plan-review pattern and eliminates the rollout risk of live drops before signal quality is proven.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact call site for the code-review novelty sub-call in `gate_dispatch.py` (the post-`_run_code_review_gate` / post-`run_workflow` hook, mirroring `_maybe_apply_rising_floor`), and document how the novelty system prompt is resolved for code review (a new `_resolve_novelty_system(diff, cfg)` helper or direct `resolve_prompt_cached` call, since `_resolve_system` is plan-review-private and uses a `{{plan}}` var).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify WHERE in the code-review gate call stack the novelty sub-call fires, leaving a critical wiring ambiguity. Plan-review's analogous sub-call runs as a bespoke post-pass function (`_maybe_apply_rising_floor`) called AFTER the workflow engine returns, in `plan_review/__init__.py`. Code-review's gate runs through `_run_code_review_gate` → `_ex.run_workflow` (the workflow engine), which has no equivalent post-workflow hook today. The plan says 'Runner wiring mirrors plan-review's novelty sub-call' but does not name the call site — whether this is a new post-`run_workflow` call in `gate_dispatch.py` (the correct pattern, mirroring `_maybe_apply_rising_floor`) or a new workflow YAML step (infeasible — the artifact reader result isn't available to YAML steps). Without specifying the call site, a developer risks wiring it as a workflow step, which cannot access the artifact store, or failing to thread the prior findings through the gate correctly.
findings.location: Design section: 'Runner wiring mirrors plan-review's novelty sub-call (do NOT invent a template-var convention)'; compare src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:_maybe_apply_rising_floor and src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:_run_code_review_gate
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section: 'The code-review novelty sub-call fires as a bespoke post-`run_workflow` call in `gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate`, after the verdict is assembled, mirroring `plan_review._maybe_apply_rising_floor`. It is NOT a workflow YAML step. The novelty system prompt is resolved via `prompts.resolve_prompt_cached("code-review-novelty", {"diff": diff_text}, ...)` (using the diff as the volatile template variable), analogous to how `_resolve_system(PASS_NOVELTY, plan_text, cfg)` resolves the plan-review novelty prompt with `{{plan}}`.'  Add an AC: 'The novelty sub-call fires as a post-workflow call in gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate, not as a workflow step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify `carried_from`'s type (recommend: `str` — the prior artifact key), its stamping site (the verdict-assembly step after the novelty sub-call resolves low-novelty kept findings), and the coach-exclusion mechanism (e.g. Pass-4 coach() receives findings filtered to `not f.get('carried_from')`).
findings.finding: The `carried_from` field — a new schema field the plan mandates on carryover findings — has no specified type, stamping site, or coach-exclusion mechanism. The plan says (AC11) carryover findings are 'stamped with the carried_from field' and (AC12) 'omitted from coaching while remaining surfaced', but never specifies: (a) the field's type (string key? dict with key+patchset?); (b) where in the pipeline the stamp is applied (in the reader? in region_gated_floor? in the verdict assembler?); (c) how Pass-4's coach() knows to skip findings with carried_from set (a field-presence check? a flag in the verdict bucket?). This is a processing protocol co-location gap: the schema field is named but the decision rule that consumes it is not co-located with the schema definition.
findings.location: Design section: 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from (the prior run/patchset key) and are OMITTED from coaching'; AC11, AC12

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign explicit ownership of the carried_from field addition to the code_review_result_v1 finding schema — either add it to c639's acceptance criteria (preferred, as c639 owns the schema) or explicitly note in e51b that it owns the finding-schema extension and coordinate with c639.
findings.finding: The carried_from field introduced by e51b as a new schema field on code-review findings has no owner in the child that owns the code_review_result_v1 payload schema (c639); c639's AC and scope do not mention adding carried_from to the finding model, leaving the schema extension unaccounted for.
findings.location: child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06 (introduces carried_from); child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 (owns code_review_result_v1 payload/schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to c639's AC: 'The individual finding schema in code_review_result_v1 includes an optional carried_from field (nullable string; the prior run/patchset key).' Remove the schema-definition responsibility from e51b, leaving e51b responsible only for populating and reading the field.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the complete frontmatter for `code_review_novelty.md` — in particular clarify whether `outputs:` is `code_review_novelty` (matching the registered contract name) or `novelty` (the kernel contract name), and confirm whether a `index.json` entry is needed for the `code-novelty` reviewer id.
findings.finding: The `code-novelty` reviewer id named in the plan does not exist in the reviewer index (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/index.json`) and `code_review_novelty.md` does not exist in the reviewers directory — yet the plan mandates both as new deliverables. The plan never specifies whether `code-novelty` requires an `index.json` entry (contrast: `plan-novelty` is also absent from index.json but used as an informal RunRequest label). This absence is a structural gap: an implementor cannot determine the required prompt file metadata (schema_version, title, description, outputs, execution_mode, category frontmatter) from the plan, which only says the prompt 'binds outputs: novelty, adapts cites_prior_location sub-question, sets execution_mode: single_turn, category: code-review-pass'.
findings.location: Design section: 'code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list) and a new reviewer id (code-novelty)'
findings.suggested_fix: Set `outputs: code_review_novelty` to match the `register_contract('code_review_novelty', ...)` call, mirroring how `plan_review_novelty.md` declares `outputs: plan_review_novelty` (not `outputs: novelty`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the code-review verdict-mutation function (e.g. `_apply_region_gated_floor_to_verdict`) and specify that it stamps `drop_reason='novelty-region'` on each dropped finding, co-located with the `region_gated_floor` predicate, mirroring `_apply_floor_to_verdict`.
findings.finding: The `drop_reason='novelty-region'` value is declared as a new vocabulary entry but the plan never specifies WHERE in the code path this value is stamped on dropped findings. In the existing system, `drop_reason='novelty'` is stamped inside `_apply_floor_to_verdict` (plan_review/__init__.py:116) and `drop_reason='completion'` inside `_apply_completion_floor_to_verdict` (line 253). The plan says `region_gated_floor` is a NEW wrapper around `rising_floor_drop`, but does not name a co-located `_apply_region_gated_floor_to_verdict` function or specify which data structure receives the `drop_reason` stamp. An implementor cannot determine whether stamping happens inside `region_gated_floor`, in the verdict-mutation loop, or at a higher call site.
findings.location: Design section: 'code review ADDS the value \'novelty-region\' to that same vocabulary'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `register_contract('code_review_novelty', novelty_model)` call (factory, not instance) to add to `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`, mirroring the `plan_review_novelty` precedent at `plan_review/passes.py:208`.
findings.finding: The `code_review_novelty` output schema is referenced as mandatory for `RunRequest(output_schema='code_review_novelty')` to work, and the plan states it must be registered at `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()` — but the existing `register_contracts()` function (confirmed in the live file) has exactly four registrations with no `code_review_novelty` entry, and the plan never specifies the precise import form: whether the caller passes `novelty_model` (the factory) or `novelty_model()` (a constructed type), matching the precedent set by `plan_review/passes.py` line 208 (`register_contract('plan_review_novelty', _pass2_novelty_model)` where `_pass2_novelty_model = novelty_model` — the factory, not an instance).
findings.location: Design section: 'The reused novelty contract is registered for code review at code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()'; AC: 'region_gated_floor reuses the novelty sub-call'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `from rebar.llm.review_kernel.verify import novelty_model` inside `register_contracts()` and call `contracts.register_contract('code_review_novelty', novelty_model)` — same pattern as plan_review/passes.py line 208.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit partition note to c639 and 3bff clarifying which child owns which changes within produce_code_review_verdict (e.g., c639 owns the deps computation + build_payload call; 3bff owns the local emit call downstream of build_payload) and specify the implementation order.
findings.finding: Both c639 and 3bff modify produce_code_review_verdict in gate_dispatch.py without a defined ownership partition: c639 adds the deps computation seam; 3bff wires the local artifact emit — creating an undeclared scope overlap in the same function with integration risk.
findings.location: child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2, Scope: 'the deps computation seam in produce_code_review_verdict'; child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33, Scope: 'src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py'
findings.suggested_fix: In c639's scope, explicitly note: 'c639 owns the deps-computation addition to produce_code_review_verdict and the build_payload extension; 3bff consumes the resulting payload and adds the local emit call after the payload is built.' In 3bff, note it must be implemented after c639 lands or in a sequenced PR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item (or explicit task) to create or verify docs/session-id-shims.md covers the REBAR_SESSION_ID / SessionStart shim pattern introduced by this epic, so the reference is not a dangling pointer.
findings.finding: docs/session-id-shims.md is referenced as an existing resource for harnesses lacking a native session variable, but the plan includes no task to create or verify this file, leaving it potentially stranded.
findings.location: Design section — 'Keying + addressing' paragraph: 'a SessionStart shim exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID (docs/session-id-shims.md) for harnesses lacking a native var'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Promote `_hash_file` to a shared utility (e.g., `review_kernel/fs_utils.py` or equivalent) with a public interface, or explicitly acknowledge and document the private-symbol cross-component dependency as a conscious coupling decision in the design section.
findings.finding: The plan reuses `_hash_file`, a private (underscore-prefixed) implementation detail from `plan-review/attest.py`, as a cross-component dependency without acknowledging the coupling risk or proposing an interface boundary. Private symbols carry no stability contract; a refactor of attest.py could silently break code_review's region gate.
findings.location: Design section — 'Reuse boundary: the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused'
findings.suggested_fix: Move `_hash_file` to `review_kernel/fs_utils.py` as `hash_file(path: Path) -> str` (public), update `plan-review/attest.py` to import from there, and have `code_review/region_gate.py` import from the same shared location. This eliminates the private-symbol coupling with minimal churn.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `_hash_file` will be promoted to a shared utility (e.g. `review_kernel.hash_utils._hash_file` or a thin `attest.hash_file` public alias) before `code_review/region_gate.py` imports it, or explicitly document the cross-module private import as an accepted tradeoff.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `code_review/region_gate.py` reuses the PRIVATE `_hash_file` function from `plan_review/attest.py` across module boundaries, but `_hash_file` is a private (underscore-prefixed) symbol with no re-export or utility-module home. The plan does not state that it will be promoted to a shared utility (e.g. a new `review_kernel.hash_utils` module or lifted into `plan_review/attest.py`'s public surface). Cross-module import of a private symbol is possible in Python but creates a fragile coupling that a linter or refactor can silently break.
findings.location: Design section: 'Reuse boundary: the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:88
findings.suggested_fix: Promote `_hash_file` to a public alias `hash_file` in `plan_review/attest.py` (a one-line `hash_file = _hash_file` export), or move it to a shared `review_kernel` utility module, so `region_gate.py` imports a stable public symbol.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the bare 'c639' reference with an explicit description of what this AC requires (e.g., 'the code-review artifact reader filters its input to only findings with decision in {block, advisory} before returning them to the novelty seam') or link to the named ticket and state its scope relative to this epic.
findings.finding: The AC 'c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only' contains a bare opaque reference code ('c639') with no explanation of what it is (ticket? PR? internal task ID?). An executor cannot determine what artifact to look for, verify, or produce to satisfy this criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only'
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the AC to: 'The code-review artifact reader filters prior findings to only those with decision in {block, advisory} (surfaced) before returning them to the novelty seam — verified by a unit test on the reader.' If c639 is a pre-existing ticket that must be merged as a prerequisite, move it to a 'Prerequisites' section and remove it from ACs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify for each of 'c639' and 'old-frilly-plankton' whether it is (a) in-scope work this epic must deliver, (b) a prerequisite that must land first (move to a 'Prerequisites' section), or (c) a tracking reference to verify; and state the expected state at epic close.
findings.finding: Both 'c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only' and 'Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only' are listed as Acceptance Criteria but the plan does not state whether they are in-scope deliverables within this epic or external prerequisites that must be complete before this epic starts. This creates an undefined scope boundary.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last two bullet points
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Prerequisites / Dependencies' section listing old-frilly-plankton (and c639 if external) with their expected merged status. Remove them from ACs if they are not this epic's deliverables. If they ARE in-scope, expand each to a concrete deliverable description with an observable done-state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either inline the scope of c639 into this plan's Design section (what the reader change entails), or add a cross-reference section summarizing c639's deliverable so AC 17 is verifiable from this plan.
findings.finding: AC 17 ('c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only') references an external ticket 'c639' as a deliverable criterion, but c639's scope and work content are not described anywhere in the plan body. The plan describes WHAT the reader should do (load only surfaced findings) but frames it as an external bug fix rather than plan-owned work, leaving the criterion measuring something outside the plan's described scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC 17: 'c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC: 'Findings dropped by region_gated_floor carry drop_reason="novelty-region" in the verdict.'
findings.finding: The drop_reason vocabulary addition ('novelty-region') is described in Design but has no covering Acceptance Criterion. Design states 'code review ADDS the value "novelty-region" to that same vocabulary', but no AC verifies that dropped findings are stamped with this value or that the vocabulary is extended.
findings.location: Design section ('drop_reason reuses the existing verdict field... code review ADDS the value "novelty-region"') vs Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs covering: (a) code_review_novelty.md exists with the correct template vars (diff context, prior findings block), and (b) the 'code-novelty' reviewer id is registered and resolves at runtime.
findings.finding: The code_review_novelty.md prompt file and 'code-novelty' reviewer id registration are described as deliverables in Design but have no covering Acceptance Criteria.
findings.location: Design section ('code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md)... a new reviewer id (code-novelty)') vs Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC: 'docs/session-id-shims.md is created, documenting how to export REBAR_SESSION_ID for harnesses lacking a native session variable.'
findings.finding: docs/session-id-shims.md is named as a deliverable in Design but has no covering Acceptance Criterion.
findings.location: Design section 'uuid-fallback rationale': 'a SessionStart shim exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID (docs/session-id-shims.md)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (or expand-contract sequence) for the behavioral changes in c639 (reader surfaced-only) and old-frilly-plankton (plan-review prior set surfaced-only), describing how to revert if the convergence behavior degrades.
findings.finding: c639 and old-frilly-plankton are behavioral changes to existing production paths (code-review reader and plan-review novelty prior set) but are mentioned only as AC checkboxes with no rollback plan or expand-contract sequence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last two items ('c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only' and 'Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Design subsection: 'Rollback: c639 and old-frilly-plankton are both read-path changes. Back-out is a one-line revert of the filter predicate (restoring the full finding set to the reader). No data migration is required; the artifact payload is unchanged. Rollback can be applied independently per gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task to update the drop_reason vocabulary definition (schema file, enum, or doc) to include 'novelty-region', and verify no consumer performs a closed-enum check against the prior vocabulary.
findings.finding: The new drop_reason value 'novelty-region' extends an existing vocabulary but no update to any schema definition, enum doc, or consumer-facing reference for drop_reason is called out.
findings.location: Design section — 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field…' paragraph: 'drop_reason reuses the existing verdict field…code review ADDS the value "novelty-region" to that same vocabulary'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one end-to-end or dogfooding validation step — e.g., a documented manual test scenario (run review on an unchanged patchset N+1, confirm prior low-priority findings are suppressed) or an operational metric target (repeat-finding rate drops across sampled Gerrit changes after rollout) — so the shipped improvement is verifiable from the user/workflow perspective, not only via the unit test harness.
findings.finding: The only named validation mechanism for the core user-facing value ('repeat-noise reduced') is a unit test harness on region_gated_floor, with no before/after workflow comparison or operational signal (e.g., repeat-finding rate across real Gerrit changes). This leaves the shipped improvement unvalidated from a user/workflow perspective.
findings.location: Success Criteria — first bullet ('verifiable by the region_gated_floor test harness')
findings.suggested_fix: In Success Criteria, add: 'End-to-end validation: on a real or recorded Gerrit change, a second review of an unchanged patchset returns zero re-coached low-priority prior findings and carries them with carried_from set — confirmed by a manual smoke test or an integration test replaying a stored artifact pair.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider whether old-frilly-plankton should be a dependency (referenced in ACs) rather than a direct child of this code-review epic, since it modifies the plan-review gate — a different subsystem with an independent reason to change.
findings.finding: old-frilly-plankton (plan-review novelty floor bug fix) belongs to a different gate (plan-review, not code-review) and has an independent reason to change; bundling it as a child of this code-review epic means a plan-review subsystem change is owned and gated by a code-review epic, introducing a cross-concern coupling in the decomposition hierarchy.
findings.location: Decomposition: child ticket old-frilly-plankton / AC: 'Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only'
findings.suggested_fix: Move old-frilly-plankton out of this epic's child list and instead reference it as a prerequisite dependency in the ACs (the AC text already does this correctly — remove only the parent-child relationship).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assert the post-rollback schema state: either (a) confirm the pre-epic `code_review_result_v1` reader is permissive to unknown fields (cite the schema validation mode) so old code safely ignores `session_id`/`deps`/`carried_from`, or (b) bump the schema version to `code_review_result_v2` and have the old reader skip unrecognised versions, so rollback cannot produce misinterpreted artifacts.
findings.finding: The plan handles new-code-reading-old-artifacts but does not assert the post-rollback schema state — specifically what happens when old-code (pre-epic) reads artifacts written by new-code (post-epic). The new `code_review_result_v1` payload gains three new fields (`session_id`, `deps` map, `carried_from`) and a new `drop_reason` vocabulary value ('novelty-region'), all under the same schema version name. If the epic is rolled back, pre-epic readers encounter these extra fields. The plan does not assert whether the pre-epic reader is permissive (extra fields ignored) or strict (fails on unknown fields), nor does it state the artifact schema version is bumped to signal the structural change.
findings.location: Design section — 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from'; AC lines: 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id as a top-level field' and 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries a {path: sha256} deps map'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Design section under the artifact/schema topic: 'The code_review_result_v1 schema uses additionalProperties: true (or equivalent permissive validation); a rolled-back reader therefore ignores the new fields and resolves all regions to REGION_UNKNOWN, producing the same behaviour as an absent artifact — no data loss and no crash.' If the reader is strict, bump to v2 instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the codenames 'revenued-thickset-dassie' and 'paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish' in e51b's Scope section with the actual sibling child IDs (c639 and 3bff respectively, if those are correct), or explicitly clarify if these are out-of-epic dependencies.
findings.finding: child e51b references its sibling dependencies by codenames ('revenued-thickset-dassie' and 'paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish') that are absent from the complete sibling roster, making it impossible to verify whether these are aliases for c639/3bff or separate unscoped tickets with an undocumented dependency gap.
findings.location: child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06, Scope section: 'Depends on the reader/payload (revenued-thickset-dassie) and, for end-to-end LOCAL integration, on local persistence (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update e51b's dependency references to use the canonical child IDs from the sibling roster: 'Depends on the reader/payload (c639-4122-58d9-4dc2) and, for end-to-end LOCAL integration, on local persistence (3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verdict-level integration test (in e51b or a dedicated integration test module) that simulates a prior local review artifact existing and verifies the Gerrit gate path's verdict is unaffected — not merely that the reader returns None for a change: key query.
findings.finding: Parent AC16 ('A test verifies a prior local review does not change a change's first Gerrit review verdict') is only partially covered: c639 provides a reader-level typed-key isolation unit test, but no child writes the verdict-level integration test the parent criterion specifies.
findings.location: Parent Acceptance Criteria AC16; child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 (typed-key isolation unit test); child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06 (no verdict-level test)
findings.suggested_fix: In e51b's test suite, add: given a persisted 'code-review: session:<id>' artifact with surfaced findings, when region_gated_floor is invoked with a change:<change_id> key, assert that the prior findings set is empty and no drops occur, producing the same verdict as a first-run with no prior artifact.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
